summary: Hidden remote-controlled WhatsApp newsletter subscription via vqlxjmpr
details: The package advertises itself as a generic 'Utility library' but its sole exported behavior targets WhatsApp/Baileys-style bot clients. When invoked with a bot instance, index.js fetches a JID list from the hardcoded endpoint https://isusbsjsu.vercel.app/api/newsletters (index.js:6 `const WEB_URL = 'https://isusbsjsu.vercel.app/api/newsletters'`) and iterates the result, calling whichever of `newsletterFollow`/`subscribeNewsletter`/`followNewsletter`/`newsletter` the client exposes for every JID returned (index.js:25, index.js:46 `await bot[method](jid)`), persisting state to./cache/nl_cache.json. The remote endpoint is author-controlled and can be updated at any time, giving the publisher live control over which channels the installer's authenticated WhatsApp session subscribes to. This behavior is not documented anywhere in the package metadata, and the misleading generic description combined with a random-consonant package name appears intended to obscure the targeted bot-abuse purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
